meaning: adj.莉ｻ諢冗噪,閾ｪ逕ｱ蜀ｳ螳夂噪
examples: Magistrates were given wider discretionary powers.
You may be eligible for a discretionary grant for your university course.
Of course, I have discretionary income.
The GOP says it would cut government spending to pre-2009 levels and cap future discretionary spending.
Surprisingly, provided the job situation, the consumer discretionary sector is burning hot right now.
